manifest.name: Playwright Browser Engine
manifest.//: A1 (plans/playwright-adoption/10-productionize-a1.md). The shell's PlaywrightProxy lazy-spawns `node dist/sidecar.js` on first chrome request, resolving bin_path via SidecarsRegistry.resolve("pa-playwright-browser"). Declaring sidecars[] only registers the (pkg_id,name)->bin_path mapping (registries/sidecars.rs register() validates the bin path + inserts into a HashMap; it NEVER spawns). There is deliberately NO mcp[] block: the kernel SidecarSupervisor auto-spawn path (lifecycle.rs start_supervised) is gated on at least one mcp[].is_long_lived() entry, so omitting mcp[] guarantees the kernel will NOT boot a competing process. The shell keeps exclusive control over when node runs.
